items.title: Force new game via MCP

items.title: CTD on loading save after MCP character replication session
items.description: ROOT CAUSE: po3_SaveUnbaker.dll crashing in NPC::LoadGame::thunk (Hooks.cpp:51) with null RCX. Not our plugin. RECOMMENDATION: Disable po3_SaveUnbaker when switching saves during MCP sessions.

history.note: Reported: CTD when loading Arya save after character replication session
Crash log analysis: po3_SaveUnbaker.dll in NPC::LoadGame::thunk with null RCX. SkyrimMCPPlugin not in call stack.
Closed as external — not our bug. Recommendation: disable po3_SaveUnbaker when switching saves.

items.title: call_papyrus_function — DispatchStaticCall fails on all functions
items.description: CallPapyrusFunction errors immediately on all tested functions (StorageUtil.debug_GetIntObjectCount — zero args, returns int). ConsoleUtil.ReadMessage caused a game freeze (that function blocks waiting for console input — red herring, not a VM crash). The catalog scan and GetScriptFunctions both work correctly, so the issue is in the DispatchStaticCall response path. Either the call returns a non-success code or the C# side isn't deserializing the Papyrus return value correctly. Suggest adding plugin-side logging around the dispatch to capture the error code from SkyrimVM::DispatchStaticCall.

history.note: Reported: call_papyrus_function fails immediately on StorageUtil.debug_GetIntObjectCount (no args, int return). ConsoleUtil.ReadMessage froze game — that function blocks on console input, not a VM issue. GetPapyrusCatalog (166 scripts) and GetScriptFunctions both work. DispatchStaticCall or C# return value marshaling is broken.

items.title: CTD on toggle_immortal_mode (TIM)
items.description: CTD when calling toggle_immortal_mode. Uses ExecuteConsoleCommand("tim") internally. May be CompileAndRun crashing on the TIM command specifically, or the static bool tracking is wrong.

history.note: Reported: CTD immediately on toggle_immortal_mode call. Occurred after successful take_snapshot. 7/15 tests had passed before crash.
Likely cause: TIM uses ExecuteConsoleCommand which goes through CompileAndRun. Need crash log to confirm.

Crash log analysis: SkyrimMCPPlugin.dll in call stack. RAX=0 null deref at offset 0x80. R14/R15 = BSScript::Internal::VirtualMachine. The tim command touches the Papyrus VM and crashes when VM has active scripts. Passed earlier test in clean interior (Crafting Hall) but crashed after combat/spawns/loot scan. Fix: replace tim with setessential toggle on player base ID — simpler command, less VM interaction.
Confirmed fixed. Clean toggle with setessential fallback when VM state is unsafe.

items.title: SetWeather name search fails — no editor IDs at runtime
items.description: GetFormEditorID() returns null for weather forms at runtime. ListWeathers returned 339 forms but with no names, editor IDs, or meaningful categories. SetWeather name search had nothing to match against.

history.note: Reported: ListWeathers categories all "other", SetWeather name search errors out
Root cause: GetFormEditorID() returns empty at runtime even with po3_Tweaks. No names or editor IDs available for weather forms.
Fix: classify from WeatherDataFlag (kSnow/kRainy/kCloudy/kPleasant) instead of editor IDs. SetWeather maps keywords to flags, prefers Skyrim.esm. Branch bugfix/009-weather-names.
Fix deployed, pending retest.
CONFIRMED FIXED: SetWeather name search works (snow, rain, clear, cloudy). ListWeathers returns 339 with proper categories from WeatherDataFlag + windSpeed values.

items.title: GetPlayerFactions returns only 1 faction
items.description: VisitFactions on Actor only visits runtime faction changes. Base factions from TESActorBaseData and ExtraFactionChanges not iterated. Level 250 character returned only 1 faction.

history.note: Reported: only 1 faction (Allegiance Faction) returned for Level 250 character
Root cause: VisitFactions only returns runtime changes. Base factions on TESActorBaseData::factions and ExtraFactionChanges not iterated.
Fix: iterate base + ExtraFactionChanges + VisitFactions with dedup via unordered_set. Branch bugfix/008-player-factions.
Fix deployed, pending retest.
CONFIRMED FIXED: 53 factions returned (was 1). College of Winterhold rank 6, Arch-Mage, Imperial Legion, Blades, Greybeards, Blood-Kin, etc.

items.title: Apply character blueprint
items.description: Import blueprint JSON selectively — AI presents available sections, user picks what to apply. Sections: race/gender, appearance (vanilla FaceGen + RaceMenu overlays/sculpts via .jslot), height/weight, skills/levels (native SetActorValue), perks (native AddPerk), spells/shouts (native AddSpell/UnlockWord), inventory/equipment (native Add/EquipItem), factions, body morphs (NiOverride Papyrus API — bypasses OBody). Smart approximation: if target game lacks an asset (hair, tattoo, color), AI uses SearchForms for fuzzy match and reports substitutions. RaceMenu integration: read .jslot from SKSE/Plugins/CharGen/, apply via CharGen.LoadCharacter() Papyrus call. Native API first — only console commands as last resort. Same blueprint JSON extends to FEAT-122 (spawn NPC) and FEAT-123 (create follower).

items.title: Spawn NPC from blueprint
items.description: Spawn a new NPC from blueprint JSON. PlaceAtMe with persist flag or quest alias for persistence. Apply appearance via native TESNPC face data + RaceMenu CharGen if available. Body morphs via NiOverride.SetBodyMorph() Papyrus calls. Stats/perks/spells/equipment via same native APIs as FEAT-121. FaceGen NIF/texture baking needed to avoid grey face — RaceMenu CharGen handles this. Detect installed frameworks (RaceMenu, NiOverride, CBBE) and adapt strategy.

items.title: Papyrus function scanner
items.description: Scan Data/Scripts/Source/*.psc files to build a registry of all native Papyrus functions per plugin. Extract function name, params, return type, script class name. Version against DLL file version.

items.title: Generic Papyrus VM call bridge
items.description: CallPapyrusFunction(className, functionName, args) action via SkyrimVM::DispatchStaticCall. Handles async callback, type conversion, error handling. Single endpoint that can call any registered Papyrus native.

history.note: VM call bridge working. ConsoleUtil.GetVersion returns 4 instantly. ReadMessage returns last console message. Deadlock fixed by calling DispatchStaticCall from pipe thread instead of game thread.

items.title: Plugin capability catalog MCP tool
items.description: GetPluginCapabilities(pluginName) returns all available functions with signatures. AI uses this to discover what actions are possible with each plugin.

items.title: Intent-based plugin routing
items.description: AI reads capability catalog and composes correct plugin function calls based on user intent. Tool descriptions guide the AI to match requests like "change my body" to OBody functions or "switch camera" to SmoothCam presets.

items.title: Per-plugin MCP tool generation
items.description: Auto-generate MCP tool definitions from scanned Papyrus functions. Each plugin gets its own tool group with proper descriptions derived from function signatures and script comments.

infrastructure.title: Event system
infrastructure.description: 17 event types via ScriptEventSourceHolder sinks. PollEvents with filters.

infrastructure.title: Load order query
infrastructure.description: GetLoadOrder + GetModFormIdPrefix for ESP/ESL index lookup.

infrastructure.title: Paged output for large responses
infrastructure.description: Add page/pageSize params to all large-output actions: GetInventory, GetKnownSpells, GetPerks, GetKnownShouts, GetNearbyObjects, PollEvents, GetNPCInventory, GetLoadOrder, ListWeathers, GetPlayerFactions, GetFollowers. Response includes totalItems, page, totalPages, hasMore. AI receives first page + metadata, decides whether to fetch additional pages based on task needs. Avoids loading 1394 spells or 708 perks into context when only a summary or specific item is needed. Default pageSize TBD — suggest 50 for spells/perks, 100 for inventory/objects. C# Protocol layer handles slicing; C++ plugin always returns full data.

history.note: Requested after token usage analysis. Sessions consuming 300-400K tokens largely due to get_known_spells (~35K tokens), get_perks (~26K tokens), and get_inventory (~12K tokens) being loaded in full even when only metadata needed.

infrastructure.title: ReadPluginConfig / WritePluginConfig tools
infrastructure.description: Generic tools for reading and writing JSON/INI config files from the SKSE plugins directory and Data folder. Enables integration with pure C++ SKSE plugins that have no Papyrus API (e.g. SmoothCam, ENB, MCM-based plugins). ReadPluginConfig(path) returns parsed JSON or INI as a structured object. WritePluginConfig(path, data) writes changes back. Path is relative to Skyrim Data folder. Changes take effect on next plugin hot-reload or game restart. Also covers: SmoothCam preset switching (SmoothCamPreset0-5.json), ENB parameter tweaks, MCM .ini settings. Discovered during SmoothCam integration exploration 2026-03-23 — SmoothCam has no console commands or Papyrus API, JSON file R/W is the only integration path.

history.note: Identified during SmoothCam exploration. SmoothCam stores presets as SmoothCamPreset0-5.json in Data/SKSE/Plugins/. Active config is SmoothCam.json. No console commands or Papyrus exports found. Config file R/W is the correct integration pattern for all no-API C++ SKSE plugins.

infrastructure.title: VPrint hook
infrastructure.description: Full console output capture for multi-line command results.

infrastructure.title: Config file
infrastructure.description: .ini or .toml for pipe name, log level, feature toggles.

infrastructure.description: Cache frequently queried data to reduce game thread blocking.

infrastructure.title: Push events via pipe
infrastructure.description: Pipe Protocol v2 — unsolicited event messages from plugin to client.

infrastructure.title: Plugin DI system
infrastructure.description: Dynamic integration with detected SKSE plugins.

infrastructure.title: Console output capture incomplete
infrastructure.description: Console commands return success but no output text. Commands like getav, getlevel, getqueststage produce useful output that cannot be captured.

history.note: Reported: execute_console_command returns no output field
Confirmed: BSString buffer + lastMessage both empty after command execution
Attempt 1: VPrint hook via write_branch<6> at SKSEPlugin_Load. CTD during script init — trampoline produced invalid jump target.
Attempt 2: Late-load hook at kDataLoaded, try write_branch<5> then fallback <6>, with try/catch. Deployed, pending test.

Attempt 2 failed: write_branch<5> installed but original pointer (7FF657E5D6AE) is invalid — ~400MB offset from target. Prologue has relative instructions that cant be relocated. CTD on first console print. Hook disabled. Need different approach: vtable swap, IAT hook, or Papyrus bridge.

ROOT CAUSE FOUND: C# ExecuteConsoleCommand was returning hardcoded message, discarding plugin response data. Plugin was capturing output correctly the entire time (confirmed in logs). One-line fix: return DeserializeResponse(data). All VPrint hook attempts were unnecessary — lastMessage capture works fine.
CONFIRMED WORKING: player.getav health -> 4015.57, player.getlevel -> 250.00, getStage 00074793 -> 100.00. Console output now flows through MCP pipe correctly.

infrastructure.title: GetScriptStats — Papyrus VM diagnostics
infrastructure.description: Access RE::BSScript::Internal::VirtualMachine to return active thread count, suspended stack count, running script names. Useful for debugging script lag and identifying heavy mods.

infrastructure.title: Paged output for large responses
infrastructure.description: PageResponse helper in ToolBase — stateless paging with page/pageSize params. Response envelope: items, page, pageSize, totalItems, totalPages, hasMore. page=0 returns summary only.

history.note: Implemented and tested. GetKnownSpells page=0 returns 1393 count in ~15 tokens. GetInventory paging confirmed with 325 items across 7 pages.

infrastructure.title: CallPapyrusFunction — DispatchStaticCall freezes or errors
infrastructure.description: ConsoleUtil.ReadMessage freezes (blocks on console input). StorageUtil.SetIntValue errors instantly. Catalog and function lookup work, but actual VM dispatch is broken.

history.note: Reported: ReadMessage caused game freeze. StorageUtil returned error. Catalog (3389 functions) and GetScriptFunctions both work correctly.

Likely causes: (1) ReadMessage is a blocking call that waits for console input, (2) StorageUtil needs a Form argument that we are not marshaling correctly from JSON, (3) DispatchStaticCall callback/promise not resolving properly.

FIXED. Root cause was deadlock: DispatchStaticCall called from game thread via TaskQueue, VM callback also needs game thread. Fix: call from pipe thread directly. ConsoleUtil.GetVersion returns 4, ReadMessage returns last console message. Instant response.

infrastructure.title: OOP refactor: unify player/NPC operations with polymorphic actor targeting
infrastructure.description: Consolidate duplicate player/NPC functions into single polymorphic versions with optional refId param. ResolveActor(refId) helper returns player if empty, specific actor if provided. Merge: GetPlayerFactions+NPC factions→GetFactions(refId?), GetInventory+GetNPCInventory→GetInventory(refId?), SetActorValue+SetActorValueOn→SetActorValue(attr,val,refId?), GetPlayerInfo+GetActorInfo+GetNPCDetailedInfo→GetActorInfo(refId?). Move ToggleGodMode/ToggleImmortalMode to PlayerQueries. Update Protocol, facade, and C# tools.

history.note: Created. Identified 4+ duplicate function pairs that should be unified per OOP/DRY. Deferred from quick cleanup to proper refactor session.
OOP refactor complete. 11/13 tests passing, zero regressions. Unified GetInventory(refId?), GetFactions(refId?), SetActorValue(attr,val,refId?). ToggleGodMode/ImmortalMode moved to PlayerQueries.

infrastructure.title: Plugin config file read/write/list
infrastructure.description: ReadPluginConfig, WritePluginConfig, ListPluginConfigs — file-based integration with pure C++ SKSE plugins. Works for SmoothCam, po3_Tweaks, ENB, any JSON/INI/TXT config.

history.note: Implemented and tested. All 3 tools passing. 18 po3 configs, 9 SmoothCam configs accessible.

infrastructure.title: SearchForms expanded to 30+ form types
infrastructure.description: Added: idle, race, faction, worldspace, cell, container, activator, furniture, door, flora, music, keyword, magiceffect, headpart, eyes, outfit, voicetype, class, combatstyle, wordofpower, scene, relationship, recipe, projectile, light, global.

history.note: Implemented. Needs game restart to test.

keyDiscoveries: Dot-notation console commands work: refId.command correctly targets NPCs.
get_nearby_npcs returns reference IDs. setessential needs base FormID, kill/prid need ref ID.
CompileAndRun needs RELOCATION_ID 441582 for Skyrim >= 1.6.1130 (ConsoleUtilSSE approach).
Event system captures everything but framework quests (NFF etc.) spam start/stop events.
Save game with 4000+ plugins takes 10+ seconds — async fire-and-forget is required.
Static linking (x64-windows-static) required — dynamic linking causes SKSE load failure.
Blueprint outputPath is essential — full blueprint JSON for modded characters is 130KB-627KB, overflows MCP context window without it.
Bulk add items (36 items, 0 failures) works reliably for character replication.
GetPerks new build returns 708 perks (was 12) — old build only iterated base perks.
GetKnownSpells new build returns 1,394 spells (was 1) — old build had broken spell iteration.
GetAppearance returns full face morphs (19 values), head parts (29 entries including HDT hair), and carry weight.
SetWeather name search fails but direct FormID works — Sky::ForceWeather() API is fine, name resolution needs fix.
UnlockDoor/LockDoor tool wrappers return 'script command not found' but raw console commands work — formatting issue.
BUG-007 CTD was po3_SaveUnbaker.dll, not our plugin — closed as external.
UnlockShout confirmed working — Disarm shout taught, count 52→55.
KillActor confirmed working — hostile rabbit killed, returns wasEssential/wasProtected flags.
GetCrosshairRef confirmed — returns actor details including race, level, essential, dead status.
DiscoverAllMapMarkers confirmed — tmm 0 then discover restored all markers.

BUG-006 was a one-line C# bug — ExecuteConsoleCommand returned hardcoded message instead of plugin response data. Plugin captured output correctly the entire time via lastMessage. All VPrint hook/trampoline/open-console attempts were chasing the wrong problem.
Pure C++ SKSE plugins (SmoothCam, ENB) can be integrated via config file R/W — no API needed. ReadPluginConfig/WritePluginConfig pattern works for any JSON/INI config.
Papyrus VM DispatchStaticCall must NOT be called from game thread (deadlock). Call from pipe thread, callback fires on game thread asynchronously.

pluginIntegrations.description: Console command execution + ReadMessage for output capture. Papyrus: ExecuteCommand, ReadMessage, SetSelectedReference, GetSelectedReference.
capabilities: Execute console commands via Papyrus VM

pluginIntegrations.description: File I/O, JSON storage, string manipulation from Papyrus. MorphManager for face morphs.
Advanced data structures (maps, arrays, databases) accessible from Papyrus. Persistent storage across saves.
capabilities: Complex data structures for quest/NPC tracking

pluginIntegrations.description: RaceMenu/SKEE — full character appearance customization. Face morphs, overlays, body morphs, sculpt data.
MCM configuration management. Read/write mod settings programmatically.
capabilities: Write mod settings without opening MCM

pluginIntegrations.description: OStim framework — scene management, animation control.
Camera system with presets. JSON config files for camera profiles.
Custom HUD widgets, boss bars, target info display.
Target lock system, directional movement.
HDT-SMP physics. Cloth/hair simulation.
Precision — accurate melee collision, hit detection.
Powerofthree's Tweaks — engine extensions, editor ID caching, condition fixes.
capabilities: Editor ID access at runtime (already enabled)

pluginIntegrations.description: Papyrus Extender — massive Papyrus function library. Actor, form, string, array, JSON, file operations.
SPID — distributes spells/perks/items to NPCs. Read-only interest — query what was distributed.
capabilities: Query what was distributed to which NPCs

pluginIntegrations.description: Experience mod — XP-based leveling instead of skill-based.
Seasons of Skyrim — seasonal world changes.
Map marker management framework.
Wheeler — action wheel UI. Per-save wheel configuration.
OAR — conditional animation replacement.
Database-style SKSE functions — extended Papyrus operations.
Custom skill trees from mods.
Display equipment on character (weapons on back, shields, etc).
Quick loot menu — Fallout 4 style loot popup.
Photo mode — freeze game, control camera, take screenshots.
Screenshot automation.
OBody — per-NPC body morph management.
Enhanced console — shows more info when clicking objects in console.
